description: Drain lifecycle UX gaps. Closes two ROADMAP P3 entries: (a) Pools table shows class routing config, not actual cluster distribution; (b) Drain strict mode for PUT routing fallback. Adds drain-progress admin endpoint, UI progress bar per draining cluster, completion detection (log + audit + metric + optional notify event), pre-drain bucket impact preview, end-to-end smoke validation, and an operator runbook explicitly documenting when drain is useful (multi-cluster placement deployments) vs not (single-cluster-per-class config). Pre-cycle walkthrough exercise (per feedback_cycle_end_to_end.md): 15 operator end-to-end steps + 4 negative paths drive scope, NOT story enumeration. Each step has a concrete UI/API/log surface; gaps go in scope, not P3 follow-ups. 7 stories.

userStories.title: Pools matrix — DataHealth iterates (cluster, pool) pairs
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the Pools table to reflect the actual per-cluster byte distribution (not just the class env routing config) so I can see the true footprint that drives drain decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite `internal/data/rados/health.go::DataHealth` to iterate `(cluster, distinct-pool)` matrix instead of `class → spec.Cluster`: for every registered cluster in `Backend.clusters` and every distinct pool referenced by `Backend.classes`, call `ClusterStatsProbe.GetPoolStats(pool)` against that cluster and emit one `PoolStatus{Cluster, Name, Class, BytesUsed, ObjectCount}` row
When multiple classes share a pool, `PoolStatus.Class` is the comma-joined sorted list of class names (matches existing memory + S3 backend convention for multi-class-per-pool aggregation)
`internal/data/s3/health.go` same shape: for every cluster × distinct bucket, emit one `PoolStatus` row with HeadBucket-derived telemetry
Memory backend (`internal/data/memory/backend.go`) keeps the single `Cluster=""` synthetic row — no change
Total row count after the refactor: `#clusters × #distinct-pools` (e.g. 2 clusters × 3 pools = 6 rows for the multi-cluster lab); previously was `#classes` rows all under `default`
Sort order: ascending by `(Cluster, Name)` — matches existing UI expectation; empty cluster sorts last

Unit test in `internal/data/rados/health_test.go` exercises a 2-cluster × 3-class fixture (or analogous) and asserts 6 rows with distinct `Cluster` values + per-cluster `BytesUsed` populated independently per cluster
Unit test for the comma-joined class label when two classes share one pool
Unit test for memory backend regression — still emits single `Cluster=""` row
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Strict mode env + PUT-only refuse + DrainRefused wire shape
userStories.description: As an operator, I want PUT chunks routing fallback to refuse draining clusters when `STRATA_DRAIN_STRICT=on` so I can guarantee no new chunks land on a cluster I'm decommissioning, while every read / delete / multipart-finalize path keeps working against the drained cluster.
acceptanceCriteria: New env `STRATA_DRAIN_STRICT` parsed in `internal/data/rados/Config` (and shared helper used by S3 if applicable); default `off`; values `on` / `off` / `true` / `false` accepted; unknown → fail-fast at boot with clear error

When `STRATA_DRAIN_STRICT=on`, `RADOS Backend.PutChunks` (and S3 symmetric path) consults the drain map after `placement.PickClusterExcluding` returns ""; if `spec.Cluster` ∈ `draining` → return new sentinel error `data.ErrDrainRefused` carrying the resolved cluster ID

Strict refusal triggers ONLY on PUT chunks (writes) — `GetChunks`, `Delete`, `Head`, multipart `Complete` / `Abort`, `List` ALL continue to work against draining clusters (drain semantic is stop-write, not stop-read)

s3api handler maps `data.ErrDrainRefused` to HTTP `503 ServiceUnavailable`, response body `DrainRefusedcluster is draining and STRATA_DRAIN_STRICT=on refuses fallback/bucket/key`, response header `Retry-After: 300`
Metric `strata_putchunks_refused_total{reason="drain_strict",cluster}` counter increments per refusal
When `STRATA_DRAIN_STRICT=off` (default), existing fail-open behavior is preserved byte-for-byte — no regression on single-cluster smoke tests
Unit test: simulated `STRATA_DRAIN_STRICT=on` + drained cluster + empty policy → `Backend.PutChunks` returns `ErrDrainRefused`; same scenario with `=off` → falls back to draining cluster as today
Integration test (two-cluster lab gated by `integration` build tag): drain `default`, set `STRATA_DRAIN_STRICT=on`, PUT to bucket without Placement → expect 503 DrainRefused with `Retry-After: 300`
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Drain progress admin endpoint + rebalance-worker manifest scan caching
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a `/drain-progress` endpoint per cluster so I can see how many chunks remain and an ETA without ssh-ing into ceph nodes.
acceptanceCriteria: New admin endpoint `GET /admin/v1/clusters/{id}/drain-progress` returns JSON `{state: "live"|"draining"|"removed", chunks_on_cluster: int|null, bytes_on_cluster: int|null, last_scan_at: RFC3339|null, eta_seconds: int|null, deregister_ready: bool|null}`
When state=live → `chunks_on_cluster=null`, `bytes_on_cluster=null`, `eta_seconds=null`, `deregister_ready=null` (only meaningful while draining)

Rebalance worker (`internal/rebalance`) extends its per-tick scan: for every draining cluster, walk all manifests via `meta.Store.ListBuckets` + `ListObjects` and count chunks where `BackendRef.Cluster == draining-id`; cache results under `progressCache map[clusterID]ProgressSnapshot{Chunks, Bytes, LastScanAt, BaseChunksAtStart, CompletionFiredAt}`
`BaseChunksAtStart` captured on the first scan after a `live → draining` transition (or directly on transition if cheap), so the UI can render percentage filled

ETA computed from chunks_remaining / move_rate, where `move_rate` is derived from the existing `strata_rebalance_chunks_moved_total{from=<id>}` Prometheus counter rate over the last 5 minutes; if move_rate is 0 → ETA = null
`deregister_ready = state == "draining" && chunks_on_cluster == 0`
Endpoint reads from `progressCache` — does NOT scan synchronously per request (would be O(buckets × objects))
Stale-cache behavior: if `last_scan_at` older than `2 × STRATA_REBALANCE_INTERVAL`, response includes `warnings: ["progress data stale"]` + raw values still returned
OpenAPI spec at `internal/adminapi/openapi.yaml` documents the new endpoint
Audit row stamped via `s3api.SetAuditOverride(ctx, "admin:GetClusterDrainProgress", "cluster:<id>", ...)`
Unit test: rebalance worker scan populates cache; endpoint returns expected counts + ETA + deregister_ready transitions

Integration test (two-cluster lab): drain cluster, PUT 5 objects via a bucket whose policy excludes drained, run rebalance ticks → assert chunks decrease, ETA shrinks, `deregister_ready` flips true at completion
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: UI progress bar on draining cluster card + deregister-ready chip + strict-mode chip
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a visual progress bar on each draining cluster card so I can see at a glance how close it is to deregister-ready, plus visual indicators when strict mode is active.
acceptanceCriteria: `web/src/components/storage/ClustersSubsection.tsx` renders a new `<DrainProgressBar>` block on cards where state=draining
TanStack Query fetches `/admin/v1/clusters/{id}/drain-progress` every 30s with query key `clusterDrainProgress(<id>)`

Bar content while draining: progress bar (filled = `1 - chunks_on_cluster / BaseChunksAtStart` when BaseChunksAtStart known; fallback shows just text when unknown), text "<X> chunks · <Y> remaining · ~<ETA>"

When `deregister_ready=true` (state=draining + chunks_on_cluster=0) → bar replaced with green chip "✓ Ready to deregister (env edit + restart)" + hover tooltip explaining the env edit + rolling restart procedure
When state=live → bar not rendered
Stale-cache warning: when response carries `warnings: ["progress data stale"]` → text rendered in muted color with "(stale)" suffix

`GET /admin/v1/clusters` response gains top-level `drain_strict: bool` field (or new diagnostics endpoint exposing the env value); when `true` → small "strict" chip appended next to every cluster's state badge; tooltip "PUTs to fallback path refused with 503 DrainRefused"
Strict chip is most relevant on draining cards but visible globally (env is a global flag); operator hint is on draining cards via tooltip
Bundle size delta ≤ 4 KiB gzipped
Reuse existing `<Progress>` / `<Badge>` / `<Tooltip>` primitives where available; new components in `web/src/components/storage/`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Completion detection + log + audit + metric + optional notify event
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a clear signal when a cluster's drain reaches zero chunks so I know it's safe to remove the cluster from env without checking ceph cli.
acceptanceCriteria: Rebalance worker compares scan-to-scan: when `chunks_on_cluster` transitions `>0 → 0` for a draining cluster, emit one INFO log `"drain complete"` with structured fields `cluster=<id>`, `scan_seconds=<duration>`, `final_bytes_moved=<n>`
Emit one audit row via `meta.Audit` with action=`drain.complete`, resource=`cluster:<id>`, `actor="system:rebalance-worker"`
Counter `strata_drain_complete_total{cluster}` increments by 1 on the transition

Idempotent: if `chunks_on_cluster` stays at 0 across N consecutive scans, the completion event fires ONCE (state held in `progressCache.CompletionFiredAt`); only re-fires if new chunks land on the cluster (e.g. via strict-mode-off PUT) and then drain again

Optional notify event: when `STRATA_NOTIFY_TARGETS` is configured, emit a `s3:Drain:Complete` event payload `{cluster, bytes_moved, completed_at}` through the existing notify worker pipeline; notify failure does NOT block the worker (best-effort)
Unit test: simulate scan transitions `5 → 3 → 0 → 0`; assert log + audit + metric fire exactly once on the `3 → 0` transition
Unit test: simulate `5 → 3 → 0 → 2 → 0` (drain → fill → drain) → completion fires twice
Integration test (two-cluster lab): full drain workflow end-to-end (drain → wait for moves → check completion fires)
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Pre-drain bucket impact preview + policy-all-drained warning
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to see which buckets reference a cluster in their Placement policy BEFORE I drain it so I'm not surprised when PUTs start landing on the wrong cluster or get refused.
acceptanceCriteria: New admin endpoint `GET /admin/v1/clusters/{id}/bucket-references` returns `{buckets: [{name, weight, chunk_count, bytes_used}], total_buckets: int, next_offset: int|null}` — backed by `meta.Store.ListBuckets` filtering on `Placement[<cluster>] > 0`, chunk_count + bytes_used pulled from the existing `bucket_stats` row
Pagination via `?limit=N&offset=M` (default limit=100); response includes `next_offset` when truncated
Sort: descending by `chunk_count`, then alphabetic on `name`
Endpoint reads from meta + bucket_stats — no manifest scan (the rebalance worker progress scan covers the actual-distribution side)
OpenAPI spec updated for the new endpoint + JSON schema
Audit row via `s3api.SetAuditOverride(ctx, "admin:GetClusterBucketReferences", "cluster:<id>", ...)`

UI: new `<BucketReferencesDrawer>` component opened from a "Show affected buckets" link on each cluster card (visible always, not only when draining); drawer lists buckets with chunk_count column + "View Placement" link to BucketDetail Placement tab

UI: Bucket Placement tab gains a `<PolicyDrainWarning>` chip rendered when EVERY cluster with non-zero weight in the current policy is `state=draining` — text "All clusters in this policy are draining. New PUTs will be refused (strict mode) or fall back to the class default (default mode). Update the policy before traffic resumes."

UI: `<ConfirmDrainModal>` (from placement-ui US-002) gets a new info row: "<N> buckets reference this cluster — view list" linking to the new drawer; clicking the link opens the drawer without closing the modal
Bundle size delta ≤ 4 KiB gzipped
Unit test for the meta filter (bucket with `Placement={c1:1,c2:1}` → matches when querying both c1 and c2; bucket without policy → matches neither)
Integration test: 3 buckets with mixed policies, query `/bucket-references` for each cluster, assert per-cluster lists match
`go vet ./...` passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: End-to-end smoke validation + docs + ROADMAP close-flip + PRD removal + operator runbook update
userStories.description: As an operator, I need every step of the drain workflow validated end-to-end against the multi-cluster lab AND documented — so I never hit a "wire field exists but nothing renders it" gap after a cycle merges.
acceptanceCriteria: **Smoke walkthrough script** at `scripts/smoke-drain-lifecycle.sh` drives every step from the User Journey Walkthrough table (15 happy-path steps + 4 negative paths) against the running `multi-cluster` compose profile; script EXITS NON-ZERO if any step fails the assertion

Smoke asserts via curl + `docker exec rados ls | wc -l` + `jq`: Pools table returns 6 rows (2 clusters × 3 pools), `/bucket-references` endpoint returns expected buckets per cluster, `/drain-progress` endpoint flips `deregister_ready=true`, strict-mode PUT returns `503 DrainRefused` with `Retry-After: 300`, default fail-open mode preserves existing behavior

Negative paths covered: (a) bucket policy `{cephb:1}` only + drain cephb + strict on → 503 DrainRefused; (b) bucket policy `{cephb:1}` only + drain cephb + strict off → fail-open to class default with WARN log; (c) Undrain mid-drain → state=live, banner gone, progress cache cleared; (d) drain on single-cluster-per-class config → docs warn that drain is stop-write only with no migration
`make smoke-drain-lifecycle` Makefile target runs the script

Playwright spec `web/e2e/drain-lifecycle.spec.ts` covers the UI half of the same 15 steps: every UI surface (drawer, progress bar, deregister chip, strict chip, policy-drain-warning) gets clicked + asserted against the in-memory test rig (no Docker for CI fast path)

`docs/site/content/best-practices/placement-rebalance.md` gains new "Drain lifecycle" section covering: (a) when drain is useful (multi-cluster placement deployments with bucket Placement policies naming ≥2 clusters), (b) when drain is NOT useful (single-cluster-per-class config — chunks have nowhere to migrate; drain becomes pure stop-write), (c) pre-drain operator checklist (open bucket-references drawer, verify hot buckets have alternate-cluster policy, verify pool names exist on target clusters, optionally flip `STRATA_DRAIN_STRICT=on`), (d) drain procedure (Show affected buckets → Drain → typed confirm → wait for progress bar → green chip → env edit + rolling restart), (e) abort/recovery (Undrain at any point), (f) walkthrough table copied verbatim from this PRD into the operator runbook

`docs/site/content/best-practices/web-ui.md` capability matrix gains rows for: Pools matrix, Bucket references drawer, Drain progress bar, Deregister-ready chip, Strict-mode chip, Policy-all-drained warning chip
Project root `CLAUDE.md` 'Background workers' section gets an updated rebalance bullet describing the new progress scan + completion semantics + `STRATA_DRAIN_STRICT` env

`ROADMAP.md` TWO entries close-flipped in the same commit: (a) `**P3 — Pools table shows class routing config, not actual cluster distribution.**` → `~~**P3 — ...**~~ — **Done.** <summary>. (commit \`<pending>\`)`; (b) `**P3 — Drain strict mode for PUT routing fallback.**` → `~~**P3 — ...**~~ — **Done.** <summary>. (commit \`<pending>\`)`; closing SHA backfilled in follow-up commit on main per CLAUDE.md convention
`tasks/prd-drain-lifecycle.md` REMOVED in the same commit (PRD lifecycle rule — Ralph snapshot is canonical)
Manual screenshot of Storage page after smoke run captured + referenced in the operator runbook (visual proof matrix renders)
`make docs-build` succeeds
`make vet` succeeds
`make test` succeeds
`pnpm run build` succeeds
`make smoke-drain-lifecycle` succeeds against the running `multi-cluster` lab
Typecheck passes
